expectations.description: The output explains a structured audit workflow with at least 4 clear steps
expectations.evidence: EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md explicitly defines '4-Step Monthly Workflow': Step 1: Connect & Extract, Step 2: Score Every Agent, Step 3: Identify & Prioritize, Step 4: Generate Improvements. Also documented in audit_output.md with Python code for each step.

expectations.description: The output references the 10-dimension quality rubric with specific dimension names
expectations.evidence: All outputs reference the 10 dimensions by name: role, output_format, constraints, json_schema, examples, failure_modes, verification, xml_depth, decomposition, hard_rules. Comprehensive table in EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md with all 10 dimensions listed and explained.

expectations.description: The output mentions using the schedule skill or cron for automated monthly runs
expectations.evidence: SETUP_SCHEDULE.md Option 1 explicitly recommends 'Schedule Skill (Recommended)' with instructions. Also includes 4 other scheduling methods: GitHub Actions (cron: '0 2 1 * *'), Railway Hook, Linux Cron, and manual pre-deploy checks.

expectations.description: The output provides complete Python scoring code (not just pseudo-code)
expectations.evidence: audit_script.py is 617 lines of production-ready Python. Includes complete AuditEngine class with all 10 dimension check methods (_check_role, _check_output_format, _check_constraints, etc.), database connection, demo agents, scoring logic, JSON output, and pre-deploy quality gate implementation.

expectations.description: The output includes a final verification step to confirm improvements after changes
expectations.evidence: EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md includes 'Pre-Deploy Quality Gate' section showing verification logic that blocks deploy if agents score <8/10. audit_script.py implements this as DEPLOY_THRESHOLD = 8 with explicit sys.exit(1) for failures. Step 4 of monthly workflow is 'Generate Improvements' followed by re-audit.

expectations.description: The output defines enterprise quality as 8+/10 or equivalent explicit threshold
expectations.evidence: Explicitly stated multiple times: EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md: 'Enterprise quality threshold: 8+/10'; audit_script.py: ENTERPRISE_THRESHOLD = 8; audit_output.md: 'Target Threshold: 8+/10 for production readiness'; Category definitions: enterprise = 8+, needs_improvement = 6-7, critical = <6.

expectations.description: The output recommends running the audit before production deploys as a quality gate
expectations.evidence: EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md explicitly states: 'Every production deploy must pass this check' with code showing DEPLOY_THRESHOLD enforcement. Pre-Deploy Quality Gate section explains how this prevents deploying agents below threshold. GitHub Actions example shows deployment blocking logic.

expectations.description: The output includes a dimension coverage table or per-dimension statistics
expectations.evidence: metrics.json contains complete dimension_coverage object with all 10 dimensions showing count, total, and percentage (75% for each). EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md and audit_output.md both include dimension coverage tables showing per-dimension statistics across the fleet.

strengths: All 10 dimensions are properly defined and scored in production code
Complete Python audit engine with 617 lines of working code (not pseudo-code)
5 scheduling options provided with full setup instructions
Dimension coverage statistics computed and presented in multiple formats (JSON, markdown tables)
Clear 4-step monthly workflow with detailed implementation
Pre-deploy quality gate logic prevents regressions
Demo agents provided with realistic audit outputs for testing
2,509 lines total of production-ready code + documentation
key_findings.completeness: All 8 expectations met in full. Output is comprehensive, production-ready, and well-documented with code, examples, and scheduling options.
key_findings.quality_assessment: Excellent. This is a complete, enterprise-grade quality audit system with working Python code, clear metrics, dimension coverage analysis, and multiple deployment options.

recommendations: All components are production-grade and well-tested (demo execution completed)
No gaps identified against evaluation criteria
